nodes.label: Sensitizing Mutation
nodes.description: Classical activating mutation: Exon 19 deletion or L858R. Constitutively activates EGFR kinase. ~85–90% of TKI-responsive NSCLC.

nodes.label: 1st / 2nd Gen TKI
nodes.description: Gefitinib, erlotinib (1st gen) or afatinib, dacomitinib (2nd gen). Competitive ATP-binding or irreversible pan-HER inhibition. Median PFS ~9–14 months.

nodes.label: T790M Acquired
nodes.description: Most common (50–60%) acquired resistance to 1st/2nd gen TKIs. Gatekeeper mutation: bulky Met side chain + increased ATP affinity. Clinical resistance typically at 9–14 months.

nodes.label: Osimertinib (3rd Gen)
nodes.description: Mutant-selective irreversible EGFR TKI. Designed to accommodate T790M. Covalently binds C797. FDA approved 2015 (T790M+); 2018 (first-line). Median first-line PFS ~18 months.

nodes.label: C797S Acquired
nodes.description: On-target resistance to osimertinib. Removes the cysteine osimertinib covalently bonds to. C797S + T790M in cis = fully resistant to all ATP-competitive TKIs.

nodes.label: PROTAC Degraders
nodes.description: Next frontier: PROTACs/BiDACs (e.g. CFT8919, Phase 1) bypass active-site resistance by destroying mutant EGFR protein via ubiquitin-proteasome system. Catalytic mechanism — sub-stoichiometric.

nodes.label: Osimertinib 1st-Line
nodes.description: Osimertinib is now preferred first-line (FLAURA trial). Delays T790M emergence. Superior CNS penetration. Median OS ~38 months.

nodes.label: C797S After 1st-Line Osimertinib
nodes.description: When osimertinib is used first-line, C797S can emerge without prior T790M (in trans with L858R or exon 19 del). Options remain limited.

nodes.label: Exon 20 Insertion
nodes.description: 4–10% of EGFR-mutant NSCLC. Intrinsically TKI-resistant. Requires different treatment approach.

nodes.label: Amivantamab
nodes.description: Bispecific EGFR/MET antibody (Rybrevant, J&J). FDA approved 2021 for EGFR exon 20 insertion NSCLC. Targets EGFR extracellularly — immune cell-directed killing, no active-site binding issue.

nodes.label: Off-Target Resistance
nodes.description: MET amplification, HER2 amplification, bypass pathway activation, histologic transformation (small cell). Accounts for ~40–50% of osimertinib resistance.

nodes.label: 4th-Gen Allosteric Inhibitors
nodes.description: EAI045, BLU-701 and related mutant-selective allosteric inhibitors targeting the inactive EGFR conformation. Active vs C797S (don't require active-site binding). In development.
